notes: distance_threshold: higher = more lenient ArcFace matching (presentation default 5.0)
confidence_override_threshold: if detection confidence exceeds this, the frame can pass even with a noisy distance
max_faces_allowed: auth is hard-aborted if this many faces appear at once
tracking_fps: how many frames per second to sample during the challenge window
embeddings_path: pre-built .pkl generated by tools/build_voice_embeddings.py
Format: {person_name: [embedding_tensor, ...]} — one tensor per training WAV.
Voice module does NOT record independently during auth.
It receives the WAV captured by CaptureSession during the lip challenge.
similarity_threshold: minimum average cosine similarity to accept identity.
Demo preset lowers the bar for noisy presentation rooms.

challenge_sentences: Hello Trigurad, verify my identity now
Hello Trigurad, Check my voice and lips
Hello Trigurad, Confirm my identity now
Hello Trigurad, Please unlock my account
notes: One sentence is picked randomly per session — user cannot predict it in advance.
match_threshold: fuzzy SequenceMatcher ratio to accept liveness (presentation default 0.30)
allow_partial_match: when true, a short intelligible transcript can still clear demo liveness
partial_match_floor: score floor used when any transcript is captured
countdown_seconds: pause between showing the sentence and starting to record
The same recording is used by both lip (VSR) and voice (speaker ID) modules.
Weights must sum to 1.0 across all three modules.
Disabled module weights are split equally among active modules.
All modules run in parallel. Lip liveness is a hard gate — if lip is enabled
and fails, auth is denied regardless of face+voice score.
